flags.description: Citation year in DB header: DB contains '2022 ND 237' on line 4 and line 18. PDF (authoritative source) contains '2023 ND 237'. The opinion was decided 2022-12-22 but the PDF is a corrected opinion filed 02/21/23 (as noted in the header 'Corrected Opinion Filed 02/21/23 by Clerk of the Supreme Court'). The PDF shows the official corrected citation. This is HIGH-RISK and flagged rather than auto-fixed because it represents a substantive correction by the court.

Docket number in caption: DB shows ' No. 20220223' (single case number). PDF shows 'Nos. 20220223 & 20220224' (both consolidated case numbers). This is not a transcription defect but rather incomplete data in the DB - the opinion consolidated two appeals but the DB caption only lists the first docket number. The PDF clearly shows both cases via 'Nos. 20220223 & 20220224'. This is a critical omission affecting the procedural posture.

coverage_note: Opinion is short (2 paragraphs on 1 page). All text examined end-to-end. Citation formatting defect fixed; citation year and docket number deficiencies flagged for human review due to their high-risk nature."

coverage_note: Comprehensive comparison of DB text against authoritative PDF source. All paragraph markers verified. Opinion text verified for character-level accuracy (excluding intentional straight-quote policy which differs from PDF's curly quotes). Layout/whitespace differences noted as non-substantive. All identified defects are OCR ingestion errors or formatting compression artifacts. The opinion structure includes intentionally-skipped paragraph numbers (¶36→¶40) present in both source and DB, with ¶37-39 appearing in corrupted form."

flags.location_quote: ¶9 (3 instances): 'a. The term includes: . . . . (6)' and 'injury. . . . . b. The term does not include:' and '. . . . (10) A mental injury'; ¶22 (1 instance): 'compensable. . . . If the legislature'
flags.description: The string '. . . .' (spaced ellipsis with 7 characters) appears 4 times in the DB text. The PDF text extraction shows the compact form '....' (4 characters) for the first 3 instances in ¶9 (structural ellipses in statute quotation), suggesting these are ingestion artifacts. However, the 4th instance in ¶22 appears as spaced '. . . .' in both DB and PDF (it is an omission mark within quoted ALJ text and may be intentionally spaced). Since all 4 occurrences are byte-identical and the verified_count requirement mandates matching the actual file count, this is flagged for human review rather than proposed. Recommend: verify that the ¶22 instance is intentionally spaced (as omission marks in a quote), then change only the 3 ¶9 instances to compact '....' format per ND Court opinion convention.

coverage_note: Comprehensive review of 2022 ND 220 (Wheeler v. Sayler, DB id 18105). All 11 paragraphs examined. All split/join errors identified and verified for byte-exact count=1. Page furniture (form feeds, page numbers, section headers spacing) are known artifacts handled separately and not modified."

proposals.source: PDF pdftotext -layout authoritative source line 138: 'Bearce v. Yellowstone Energy Dev., LLC, 2019 ND 89, ¶ 20, 924 N.W.2d 791. "A'
proposals.evidence: DB text has incorrect line breaks within the Bearce citation (lines 151-154 of DB), splitting the reporter citation across multiple lines. The authoritative PDF shows the complete citation on a single line with proper punctuation. This is an OCR/ingestion artifact where newlines were incorrectly inserted mid-citation.
proposals.note: The citation appears in ¶9 discussing the legal standard for novation.

proposals.evidence: DB text has incorrect line breaks within the Welch citation (around lines 159-163 of DB), splitting the reporter citation across multiple lines with spurious newlines. The authoritative PDF shows the complete citation as a single line. This is the same type of OCR/ingestion artifact as the Bearce citation.
proposals.note: The citation appears in ¶9, immediately after the Bearce citation, discussing the standard of review for findings of fact.

coverage_note: Opinion 2022 ND 223 (DB id 18102) is a short summary disposition. All content sections of the opinion have been examined. Four split_join defects were identified (unwanted line breaks within citations and after citations). No OCR character errors, digit errors, missing text, or heading/paragraph sequence anomalies detected. Whitespace layout variations in the header section (PDF's column/margin formatting) are not content defects and are properly ignored per protocol."

Straight vs. curly quotes policy applied (not flagged or proposed). All high-risk elements (case numbers, party names, citations, rule references) verified consistent with source PDF."

flags.location_quote: All instances of quotation marks (' ' and " ") throughout document
flags.description: PDF uses curly/typographic quotation marks; DB uses straight ASCII quotes. This divergence is INTENTIONAL per policy and is not proposed as a defect.

flags.location_quote: Page breaks indicated by form feed character (0x0C) before [¶3] and other locations
flags.description: PDF source contains 4 form feed characters indicating page breaks; these are handled by a separate deterministic pass per rules and should not be proposed for removal.

coverage_note: Opinion 2022 ND 206 (State v. Peltier) verified against PDF source. All text content verified to match between DB and source. Three citation formatting defects identified where PDF ingestion/OCR split valid citations across multiple lines. No other content errors detected. Remaining differences are structural (whitespace/layout) and intentional policy (quote style)."

proposals.source: PDF pdftotext extraction confirms 'GMR' at line 172 of authoritative source
proposals.note: The defendant company is GMR Transportation (not GMD). This is a case citation (2015 ND 121) appearing at paragraph 10 of Section III. The error is a single-character OCR misread: D→R in the company name. Verified against PDF text which clearly shows 'GMR Transp'.

flags.location_quote: Line 8 of PDF (between caption and opinion body)
flags.description: PDF contains 'Corrected Opinion Filed 03/01/23 by Clerk of the Supreme Court' notice, not present in DB. This is a metadata/notice line appearing after the FILED header block and before the IN THE SUPREME COURT caption. It indicates the opinion was corrected on 03/01/23.

flags.location_quote: Page 4 of PDF, line 188 (in footnote area)
flags.description: PDF contains page footer 'Filed by Clerk of Supreme Court 03/01/23' appearing between ¶11 and ¶11 text continuation. This is a page break artifact (form feed at position with running footer) and is not part of the opinion body.

DB contains 14 runs of 3+ blank lines; PDF uses form feeds (0x0C) at page breaks instead. The DB shows more blank spacing in sections marked by 'I', 'II', 'III', etc. (before section headings). PDF has 10 form feeds. This is normal OCR/ingestion artifact—the DB expanded form feed page breaks into blank lines.

coverage_note: Complete word-by-word review of opinion body comparing DB against authoritative PDF. Citation verification included. The 'Corrected Opinion' and page footer are metadata/formatting artifacts that fall outside the opinion body text itself and are not proposed as content changes."
